articles.title: IMD forecasts more heatwaves in coastal Andhra Pradesh, warmer nights across State - The Hindu
articles.search_term: heatwave (Anantapur OR Chittoor OR "East Godavari" OR Guntur OR Krishna OR Kurnool OR Nellore OR Prakasam OR Srikakulam OR Visakhapatnam OR Vizianagaram OR "West Godavari" OR "YSR Kadapa") Andhra Pradesh
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD), in its monthly outlook for May, has predicted a higher number of heatwaves in coastal Andhra Pradesh and above-normal minimum temperatures across the State this month.
Although May is typically the hottest month in Andhra Pradesh, a few areas in the northern region may see slight relief, with maximum temperatures likely to remain normal to below-normal. However, most other parts of the State are expected to experience normal to above-normal maximum temperatures.
According to the report, there is a 33.5% to 45% probability of Rayalaseema witnessing above-normal maximum temperatures. In contrast, parts of Central and North Andhra Pradesh may record normal to below-normal maximum temperatures, with a similar probability range of 33.5% to 45%.
Meanwhile, minimum temperatures across the State, barring a few exceptions, are likely to remain above-normal this month, the IMD said.
The outlook also indicates a 33.5% to 45% probability of above-normal rainfall in the State. “Usually, Andhra Pradesh does not receive much rainfall in May. Even if rainfall turns out to be normal or slightly above-normal this time, the overall quantum will remain limited,” said IMD-Amaravati Director S. Stella. She cautioned that any dip in maximum temperatures would be marginal and confined to a few areas, emphasising that the peak summer phase is not over yet.
On Saturday, the highest temperature in the State was recorded at 45.4°C in Kopperapadu of Prakasam district. Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority(APSDMA) Managing Director Prakhar Jain said 239 mandals experienced heatwave conditions, most of them in Kadapa, SPSR Nellore, Tirupati, Palnadu, Prakasam, Markapuram, Nandyal, Kurnool and Chittoor districts, where temperatures exceeded 41°C. On Sunday, heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in 124 mandals.
Published - May 02, 2026 06:22 pm IST
This is a premium article, available exclusively to subscribers.

articles.title: Heatwave Warning For 64 Mandals In Andhra Pradesh
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Andhra Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Amaravati: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority on Saturday warned of heatwave conditions and high temperatures in several mandals across the state. Heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in 64 mandals across Vizianagaram, Manyam, Polavaram, NTR, Guntur, Palnadu, Markapuram, Prakasam, Nellore, and Tirupati districts.
"Heatwave conditions along with high temperatures are likely to prevail in several mandals across the state today, with thunderstorms accompanied by lightning in some areas," APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain stated.
Jain further said that people have been advised to take necessary precautions against extreme heat and humid conditions prevailing across the state. Meanwhile, he noted that on Friday, severe heat impact was observed in 252 mandals across 20 districts, with temperatures exceeding 41 degrees Celsius.
The highest temperature recorded was 45.9 degrees Celsius in Piduguralla in Palnadu district, followed by 45.5 degrees in Gudur in Nellore district, and 45.1 degrees in Pedaparupudi in Krishna district and Torragadipadu in NTR district.
Temperature also touched 44.1 degrees in Chebrolu in Guntur district, 44 degrees in Karamchedu in Bapatla district, 43.2 degrees in Sanjamala in Nandyal district, and Kodur in Kadapa district.
Meanwhile, a weather trough extending from South Odisha through Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and Tamil Nadu up to the Gulf of Mannar continues to influence weather conditions across the state, he said.
This system is expected to bring light rains accompanied by lightning at isolated places in Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Manyam, Alluri, Polavaram, Visakhapatnam, Chittoor and Tirupati districts on Saturday. A slight reprieve is expected on Sunday and Monday as cloudy skies and rains accompanied by gusty winds are likely in some districts.
The APSDMA advised people to take precautions against extreme heat, while farmers, labourers, and shepherds were cautioned against standing under trees during thunderstorms.

APSDMA warns of heatwave in 64 mandals across Andhra Pradesh
The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority issued a heatwave warning for 64 mandals across multiple districts on Saturday. Temperatures crossed 45 degrees Celsius in some areas, while isolated rain and thunderstorms are likely in parts of the State over the weekend
Amaravati: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) on Saturday warned of heatwave conditions and high temperatures in several mandals across the State.
Heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in 64 mandals across Vizianagaram, Manyam, Polavaram, NTR, Guntur, Palnadu, Markapuram, Prakasam, Nellore and Tirupati districts.
“Heatwave conditions, along with high temperatures, are likely to prevail in several mandals across the State today, with thunderstorms accompanied by lightning in some areas,” APSDMA Managing Director Prakhar Jain said in an official press release.
Jain further said that people had been advised to take necessary precautions against extreme heat and humid conditions prevailing across the State.
Meanwhile, he noted that on Friday, severe heat impact was observed in 252 mandals across 20 districts, with temperatures exceeding 41 degrees Celsius.
The highest temperature recorded was 45.9 degrees Celsius in Piduguralla in Palnadu district, followed by 45.5 degrees in Gudur in Nellore district, and 45.1 degrees in Pedaparupudi in Krishna district and Torragadipadu in NTR district.
Temperatures also touched 44.1 degrees in Chebrolu in Guntur district, 44 degrees in Karamchedu in Bapatla district, and 43.2 degrees in Sanjamala in Nandyal district and Kodur in Kadapa district.
Meanwhile, a weather trough extending from South Odisha through Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu up to the Gulf of Mannar continues to influence weather conditions across the State, he said.
This system is expected to bring light rain accompanied by lightning at isolated places in Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Manyam, Alluri, Polavaram, Visakhapatnam, Chittoor and Tirupati districts on Saturday.
A slight reprieve is expected on Sunday and Monday as cloudy skies and rain accompanied by gusty winds are likely in some districts.
The APSDMA advised people to take precautions against extreme heat, while farmers, labourers and shepherds were cautioned against standing under trees during thunderstorms.
